summary: 16 film announcements, 14 TV announcements across major entertainment trades.

film.title: Grace
film.announcement: Millicent Simmonds co-writes and stars in the crime thriller marking Inevitable Studios' first feature, directed by Ari Costa and Eren Celeboglu.

film.title: Egg Baby
film.announcement: Amazon MGM Studios has picked up the romantic comedy from director Natalia Anderson for worldwide release on Prime Video.

film.title: Thud
film.talent: Sarah Michelle Gellar (Grim Reaper), Rudy Pankow (the Devil)
film.announcement: Sarah Michelle Gellar and Rudy Pankow have been cast in director Mali Elfman's supernatural romance based on an original screenplay.

film.title: Monsanto Courtroom Drama
film.talent: Jonathan Bailey, Laura Dern, LaKeith Stanfield, David Duchovny, Julia McDermott, Melonie Diaz, Bilal Hasna, Greg Kinnear
film.announcement: Director John Lee Hancock's Netflix courtroom drama on the 2018 Monsanto legal case has added six cast members to the ensemble.

film.title: Lady Macbeth
film.announcement: Kari Skogland and Curious Gremlin are developing a feature adaptation of Ava Reid's gothic feminist retelling of Shakespeare's Macbeth, with Emma Donoghue adapting the screenplay.

film.title: The Midnight Library
film.announcement: Paramount has won the U.S. bidding war for the adaptation of Matt Haig's novel following Cannes Film Festival buzz.

film.title: Corpus
film.announcement: Yellow Veil Pictures has boarded the NYC nightlife thriller set in the 1990s for world sales, with a premiere planned at Fantasia International Film Festival.

film.title: Black Diamond
film.announcement: The folk horror comic book series is being adapted into a feature film with Ernest Dickerson directing.

film.title: Shatter Me
film.announcement: Warner Bros. has acquired the rights to adapt Tahereh Mafi's bestselling dystopian YA series, which has become a BookTok phenomenon with over 15 million copies sold.

film.title: Shots! Shots! Shots!
film.announcement: Amazon MGM Studios has acquired the action comedy previously in development at Universal Studios, starring Jason Momoa.

film.title: Untitled Jan. 6 Friendship Drama
film.talent: Bradley Cooper (lead, in talks), Sean Penn (director)
film.announcement: Sean Penn is directing a friendship drama featuring a police officer who ends up at Jan. 6, with Bradley Cooper in talks for the lead role and Warner Bros. negotiating acquisition.

film.title: Narnia
film.announcement: Sony Pictures will distribute Greta Gerwig's Netflix-produced Narnia film internationally in theaters, arriving in February.

film.title: Mickey's Home Alone
film.announcement: Disney has announced an animated 2026 Christmas special inspired by the Home Alone films, featuring Mickey Mouse preparing for Christmas.

film.title: Hexed
film.announcement: Disney's witch-filled magical adventure animated film starring Hailee Steinfeld will land in theaters this Thanksgiving.

film.title: Shrek 5
film.talent: Mike Myers (voice), Eddie Murphy (voice), Cameron Diaz (voice), Zendaya (voice)
film.announcement: DreamWorks Animation's Shrek sequel arrives next summer with the original voice cast and Zendaya joining the ensemble.

tv.title: The Audicity
tv.announcement: Miriam Shor has joined Season 2 of AMC's The Audicity, playing FBI Agent Randy Flemming, a character introduced in the Season 1 finale.

tv.title: Heartstopper Forever
tv.announcement: Netflix has released the trailer for Heartstopper Forever, the finale feature film concluding the love story as Nick and Charlie face going their separate ways, arriving July 17.

tv.title: Funny AF with Kevin Hart
tv.announcement: Netflix has renewed the comedy competition series for a second season following Ron Taylor's inaugural winner victory at Netflix Is a Joke Fest 2026.

tv.title: The Pinnacle
tv.announcement: Hulu has put in development The Pinnacle, a high-end New York real estate drama written by Damon Cardasis and Jamieson Baker with George Stephanopoulos and Ali Wentworth as producers.

tv.title: Dirty
tv.talent: Phoebe Dynevor (Detective Eva Bennett), Samantha Morton
tv.announcement: Samantha Morton has been cast alongside Phoebe Dynevor in Matt Charman's Amazon crime thriller about a trainee detective returning to Manchester.

tv.title: Count My Lies
tv.announcement: François Arnaud has joined the cast of Hulu's limited series Count My Lies in a recurring role as Danny, friend of Lohan's character Violet.

tv.title: My Adventures with Superman: Green Lantern Spinoff
tv.announcement: Auliʻi Cravalho has been cast as Jessica Cruz in a Green Lantern spinoff stemming from My Adventures with Superman, debuting in the upcoming episode.

tv.title: Ridiculous
tv.announcement: Louis C.K.'s comedy special Ridiculous will debut on Netflix on June 30, marking his return to streaming after 9 years.

tv.title: Gomorrah - The Origins
tv.announcement: The Italian prequel series won Best Creation at the Monte-Carlo TV Festival's Golden Nymphs awards.

tv.title: The Uniform
tv.announcement: The Danish drama series won Best Series at the Monte-Carlo TV Festival's Golden Nymphs awards ceremony.

tv.title: The Testaments
tv.announcement: The Handmaid's Tale spinoff series features production design influenced by fascist architecture and stars Lucy Halliday in a breakthrough role.

tv.title: The Season
tv.announcement: Hulu's high-society Hong Kong drama from SK Global (Crazy Rich Asians studio) follows a young American woman entering the city's ultra-elite world.

tv.title: Universal Global Television (formerly UCP & UIS merger)
tv.announcement: NBCUniversal is consolidating UCP and Universal International Studios into Universal Global Television under Beatrice Springborn, eliminating 22 roles across divisions.
